reviews.review_msg: great professor but there was alot of content near the end of the course and it was not clear how we would be tested on it. Provided lots of study questions and resources for studying. Midterm was straight forward but the final was very hard. If you wanna do well ask her questions when ur stuck & go to every ta office hour (VERY helpful)

She is really passionate about biomechanics. Participation was not mandatory however I enjoyed the lectures a lot and so I always felt motivated to go to them anyways. The midterm is really straightforward but the final exam is definitely more challenging. I found all in class and tutorial material to be enough for me.

Prof. Lee is an excellent professor. She's flexible and receptive. The course was in person and on zoom. All lectures were also recorded and available for studying. She really takes the time to stop and get feedback during lectures. Exams were straightforward and clear. Everything on the test was introduced several times in lectures, and tutorials.

Professor Lee was a great teacher and provided a hybrid form of learning, allowing for students to access material and watch lectures from their own computers. Professor Lee was very receptive and open to corrections and questions during lectures, and was often fast when responding to emails. Her exams were very content based.

He has good ratings but I didn't like him personally. The midterms were fine they were reflective of the practice exams but the final was extremely hard and long which brought my grade down a lot. He was also absent a lot and wasn't even here the day of the final.
He was often absent, hard to reach, no practice final exam, no prep.
Didn't feel like he was very present as a prof this semester..

Very straightforward with exams and assignments, I recommend taking this with him. Very long lecture slides but they do provide lots of information. Tutorials were helpful to solve more practice problems and he also provided practice exams which were amazing. The assignments were somehow more difficult than the midterms but just prepare well!
Max teaches the concepts and the tutorial sections go over the quantitative questions. He is nice and very open to questions. 201 is great with him.
Do not take his course unless you want to teach yourself everything. His lectures are as if he is seeing the material for the first time. He often contradicts himself and seems confused.

Max is a nice guy, he's willing to answer questions. However, the content that he teaches in lecture is not very helpful for assignments and tests. His lectures are devoted to teaching concepts and theory however when it comes to solving physics, he rarely does it in class.

BRO JUST take it with him! he does not assume you know everything but rather he teaches you everything whether u know it or not. AND PLEASE GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS HE IS AWSOME and doesn't judge even if u have no idea what anything is ( AKA MOMENTS) :) the best prof fr fr

I love Prof max he is hands down one of my fav profs this sem. Super nice and caring and always ready to answer all of your questions and if you need help he is more than happy to give you all the help you need. Exams are super fair and everything he taught in lec. 10/10 take this class with him if u have a chance u will not regret it

Don't be deterred by the other ratings here. For 201, James was somewhat disinterested in the material and skipped by things quickly. That being said his exams were easy and related to the most obvious slides and study questions. 301 was a great class that I learned a lot in. James was very generous in marking and helpful throughout.

James was a really nice guy but seemed very uninterested in the course. He was using the slides and tests from another prof so the concepts emphasized in class were not necessarily emphasized on the exam. I gained more knowledge from my tutorial than I ever did in class so lectures felt pointless. We were only graded on exams so study constantly.

Despite his apparent knowledge of the content, he doesn't go in depth on the material that comes up on the exams and assignments. There's no textbook to refer to for this course when you're left clueless during assignments. Attend labs in order to gain a better understanding of the calculation questions.

Steve is a great prof and a great person. He does go through lectures slowly and occasionally gets lost but overall his lectures are good. he also record all lectures. if you study well you will do well.
All over the place with bad explanations

A great teacher among the BPK department. He might have somewhat of a monotonous voice, however the lectures are very concise and clear themselves that make up for a rather lengthy lecture. All the questions were very fair and the expectations were not that high. If you can tolerate basic physics, this course is highly worth it with him!

I believe that this class had the potential to be amazing, but it ended up like hell, as I feel we barely had any practice questions for exams. As a result, I found the exams to be extremely difficult, as this class was basically physics, which requires lots of practice questions.

Tony is a bit all over the place, but I really enjoyed having him as a prof! He's funny, makes things interesting, and obviously loves what he teaches. Attendance is a must due to iclicker. Biomechanics is my favourite course I've taken to date!

I clickers for marks, midterms and finals are fair if you study all the practice questions he gives u (some are way harder than what he actually tests u on) and main theory concepts discussed in class. Mt1 is calculation heavy, mt2 is theory heavy, fin (cummulative) is combo of both. Assignments can be difficult but just ask the TA for help!!

Exams were straightforward and questions were taken directly from tutorials or his slides. Super easy. Tony is hilarious too, and is available for help outside of class so if you're struggling I recommend you email him or see him in office hours.

Tony is able to deliver contents very concisely, even though every topic seems daunting at first. His exams are very fair, and he also has a ton of practice questions and problem sets which can ensure your success. His lectures are hilarious and he also gives out what is going to be on the midterms which is very helpful.

Tony's lectures seemed really easy but his exams were brutal. His explanations and demonstrations did not make sense. The first few weeks of lecture were very slow-paced but after the first midterm, he rushed through every topic. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS.

Tony is incredibly knowledgable about biomechanics. His course is organized and fair. Some topics can be hard and not as Intuitive but he provides every resource possible. He's caring and you can tell the material is part of his daily life, examples and explanations come to him naturally and lectures are super smooth. I enjoyed this course a lot!
